testcase.name: test_read

failure.message: aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host www.ine.es:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate in certificate chain (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.failure:
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x10355ca90>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x107682210> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'www.ine.es', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x106ffe180>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout > return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1313: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1112: in create_connection transport, protocol = await self._create_connection_transport( /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1145: in _create_connection_transport await waiter /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:575: in _on_handshake_complete raise handshake_exc /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:557: in _do_handshake self._sslobj.do_handshake() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ssl.SSLObject object at 0x10717b310>
def do_handshake(self): """Start the SSL/TLS handshake.""" > self._sslobj.do_handshake() E ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate in certificate chain (_ssl.c:1006)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:979: SSLCertVerificationError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_read(): """Check if a URL is loaded into a string variable.""" > pc_axis = await async_read( 'https://www.ine.es/jaxiT3/files/es/1001.px', 'iso-8859-15')
pyaxis/test/integration/test_pyaxis.py:39: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyaxis/test/integration/test_pyaxis.py:26: in async_read return await fetch(session, url, encoding) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyaxis/test/integration/test_pyaxis.py:19: in fetch async with session.get(url) as response: .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:1510: in __aenter__ self._resp: _RetType = await self._coro ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:779: in _request resp = await handler(req) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:734: in _connect_and_send_request conn = await self._connector.connect( .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:672: in connect proto = await self._create_connection(req, traces, timeout) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1239: in _create_connection _, proto = await self._create_direct_connection(req, traces, timeout) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1611: in _create_direct_connection raise last_exc .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1580: in _create_direct_connection transp, proto = await self._wrap_create_connection( _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x10355ca90>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x107682210> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'www.ine.es', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x106ffe180>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host www.ine.es:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate in certificate chain (_ssl.c:1006)')]
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1315: ClientConnectorCertificateError

@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_metadata_extract(): """Should extract pcaxis metadata into a list.""" > pc_axis = await async_read( 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi/files/_px/es/px/t20/e301/matri/a2000/l0/' '14001.px?nocab=1', 'iso-8859-15')

self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x107c5d250>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x107c5e510> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'www.ine.es', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x106ffe180>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host www.ine.es:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate in certificate chain (_ssl.c:1006)')]

@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_get_dimensions(): """Should return two lists (dimension names and members).""" > pc_axis = await async_read( 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi/files/_px/es/px/t20/e301/matri/a2000/l0/' '14001.px?nocab=1', 'iso-8859-15')

uri = 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi/files/_px/es/px/t20/e301/matri/a2000/l0/14001.px?nocab=1' encoding = 'ISO-8859-15', timeout = 10
async def read(uri, encoding, timeout=10): """Read a text file from file system or URL.
Args: uri (str): file name or URL encoding (str): charset encoding timeout (int): request timeout; optional
Returns: raw_pcaxis (str): file contents.
""" raw_pcaxis = ''
if uri_type(uri) == 'URL': try: async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: > async with session.get(uri, timeout=timeout) as response:

@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse(): """Should parse a pc-axis into a dataframe and a metadata dictionary""" > parsed_pcaxis = await pyaxis.parse( 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi/files/_px/es/px/t20/e301/matri/a2000/l0/' '14001.px?nocab=1', encoding='ISO-8859-15')

uri = 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi/files/_px/es/px/t20/e301/matri/a2000/l0/14001.px?nocab=1' encoding = 'ISO-8859-15', timeout = 10
async def read(uri, encoding, timeout=10): """Read a text file from file system or URL.
Args: uri (str): file name or URL encoding (str): charset encoding timeout (int): request timeout; optional
Returns: raw_pcaxis (str): file contents.
""" raw_pcaxis = ''
if uri_type(uri) == 'URL': try: async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: async with session.get(uri, timeout=timeout) as response: response.raise_for_status() response.encoding = encoding raw_pcaxis = await response.text() > except aiohttp.ClientTimeout as connect_timeout: E TypeError: catching classes that do not inherit from BaseException is not allowed

async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host www.ine.es:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate in certificate chain (_ssl.c:1006)')]

@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_http_error(): """Using parse() with a non-existent URL should return a 404.""" url = 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi' with pytest.raises(aiohttp.ClientResponseError): > await pyaxis.parse(url, encoding='windows-1252')

uri = 'https://www.ine.es/jaxi', encoding = 'windows-1252', timeout = 10
async def read(uri, encoding, timeout=10): """Read a text file from file system or URL.
Args: uri (str): file name or URL encoding (str): charset encoding timeout (int): request timeout; optional
Returns: raw_pcaxis (str): file contents.
""" raw_pcaxis = ''
if uri_type(uri) == 'URL': try: async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: async with session.get(uri, timeout=timeout) as response: response.raise_for_status() response.encoding = encoding raw_pcaxis = await response.text() > except aiohttp.ClientTimeout as connect_timeout: E TypeError: catching classes that do not inherit from BaseException is not allowed

async def _create_direct_connection( self, req: ClientRequest, traces: List["Trace"], timeout: "ClientTimeout", *, client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: sslcontext = self._get_ssl_context(req) fingerprint = self._get_fingerprint(req)
host = req.url.raw_host assert host is not None # Replace multiple trailing dots with a single one. # A trailing dot is only present for fully-qualified domain names. # See https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/pull/7364. if host.endswith(".."): host = host.rstrip(".") + "." port = req.port assert port is not None try: # Cancelling this lookup should not cancel the underlying lookup # or else the cancel event will get broadcast to all the waiters # across all connections. > hosts = await self._resolve_host(host, port, traces=traces) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

host = 'www.ine.net', port = 443, family = <AddressFamily.AF_UNSPEC: 0> type = <SocketKind.SOCK_STREAM: 1>, proto = 0 flags = <AddressInfo.AI_ADDRCONFIG: 1024>
def getaddrinfo(host, port, family=0, type=0, proto=0, flags=0): """Resolve host and port into list of address info entries.
Translate the host/port argument into a sequence of 5-tuples that contain all the necessary arguments for creating a socket connected to that service. host is a domain name, a string representation of an IPv4/v6 address or None. port is a string service name such as 'http', a numeric port number or None. By passing None as the value of host and port, you can pass NULL to the underlying C API.
The family, type and proto arguments can be optionally specified in order to narrow the list of addresses returned. Passing zero as a value for each of these arguments selects the full range of results. """ # We override this function since we want to translate the numeric family # and socket type values to enum constants. addrlist = [] > for res in _socket.getaddrinfo(host, port, family, type, proto, flags): ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E socket.gaierror: [Errno 8] nodename nor servname provided, or not known

async def _create_direct_connection( self, req: ClientRequest, traces: List["Trace"], timeout: "ClientTimeout", *, client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: sslcontext = self._get_ssl_context(req) fingerprint = self._get_fingerprint(req)
host = req.url.raw_host assert host is not None # Replace multiple trailing dots with a single one. # A trailing dot is only present for fully-qualified domain names. # See https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/pull/7364. if host.endswith(".."): host = host.rstrip(".") + "." port = req.port assert port is not None try: # Cancelling this lookup should not cancel the underlying lookup # or else the cancel event will get broadcast to all the waiters # across all connections. hosts = await self._resolve_host(host, port, traces=traces) except OSError as exc: if exc.errno is None and isinstance(exc, asyncio.TimeoutError): raise # in case of proxy it is not ClientProxyConnectionError # it is problem of resolving proxy ip itself > raise ClientConnectorDNSError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorDNSError: Cannot connect to host www.ine.net:443 ssl:default [nodename nor servname provided, or not known]

@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_connection_error(): """Using parse() with a wrong URL should return a connection error.""" url = 'https://www.ine.net/jaxiT3/files/t/es/px/1001.px'

""" raw_pcaxis = ''

@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse(): """Should parse a pc-axis into a dataframe and a metadata dictionary""" parsed_pcaxis = await pyaxis.parse( data_path + '14001.px', encoding='ISO-8859-15') > assert parsed_pcaxis['DATA'].dtypes['DATA'] == 'object' E AssertionError: assert <StringDtype(storage='python', na_value=nan)> == 'object'
